title: Log tamper detection via write-once-read-many chain-of-custody
exclusions: Does NOT detect insider exfiltration of log content or modifications outside the WORM store; see UC-22.35.3 (replication) and UC-22.41.3 (KMS rotation).
evidence: Saved search 'worm_chain_of_custody' runs every 15 minutes and writes hash-chain verification results to the compliance_summary index; dashboard 'Audit log tamper evidence — 30d' surfaces any unverified buckets.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.32 requires 'integrity ... of processing systems'; cryptographic hash-chain verification of audit storage is one of the standard technical measures cited by EDPB guidance on log integrity.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.32 (Security of processing) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.35.2: Log tamper detection via write-once-read-many chain-of-custody.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.35.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: §164.312(c)(1) Integrity requires mechanisms to corroborate that ePHI has not been altered. Hash-chain verification over the audit store is a direct implementation.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.312(c)(1) (Integrity) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.35.2: Log tamper detection via write-once-read-many chain-of-custody.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When an indexer's primary bucket hash ceases to match the replicated peer's hash, the UC fires within one search interval and records the bucket_id, index, and divergence timestamp.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During normal hot-to-warm rolling (which re-hashes but preserves chain continuity) the UC does NOT fire.

spl:
| rest /services/cluster/master/buckets splunk_server=* | eval primary_hash=coalesce('primaries{}.bucket_flags.hash','bucket_hash') | stats values(primary_hash) as hashes dc(primary_hash) as uniques by index, bucket_id | where uniques>1
| eval divergence_detected_at=strftime(now(),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| table divergence_detected_at index bucket_id hashes
description: Compares the cryptographic bucket hash across indexer peer nodes and raises a finding whenever the primary and its replicas disagree. A divergence is the earliest evidence of on-disk tampering or a replication-layer fault — both of which break the audit chain-of-custody that GDPR, HIPAA, PCI DSS, SOC 2, and SOX ITGC assume.
value: Produces defensible chain-of-custody evidence: auditors can be shown a 30-day view in which every bucket was either green or had a timestamped remediation ticket.
implementation: (1) Enable indexer-cluster replication with searchFactor≥2, replicationFactor≥3; (2) schedule the REST probe every 5 minutes; (3) pipe hits to compliance_summary and to ITSI as a KPI; (4) escalate to CISO on any bucket divergence older than 30 minutes.
visualization: Heat-map of indexes × buckets over 30d (green/red); single value for '% of buckets verified'; time-chart of open divergences.
knownFalsePositives: Planned rolling restarts of indexer peers can briefly show hash mismatches during re-balancing. Use maintenance_windows.csv and a 10-minute grace window.
grandmaExplanation: We check on compares the cryptographic bucket hash across indexer peer nodes and raises a finding whenever the primary and its replicas disagree. a divergence is the earliest evidence of on-disk tampering or a. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enable indexer-cluster replication with searchFactor≥2, replicationFactor≥3; (2) schedule the REST probe every 5 minutes; (3) pipe hits to compliance_summary and to ITSI as a KPI; (4) escalate to CISO on any bucket divergence older than 30 minutes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/cluster/master/buckets splunk_server=* | eval primary_hash=coalesce('primaries{}.bucket_flags.hash','bucket_hash') | stats values(primary_hash) as hashes dc(primary_hash) as uniques by index, bucket_id | where uniques>1
| eval divergence_detected_at=strftime(now(),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| table divergence_detected_at index bucket_id hashes
```

Understanding this SPL

**Log tamper detection via write-once-read-many chain-of-custody** — Produces defensible chain-of-custody evidence: auditors can be shown a 30-day view in which every bucket was either green or had a timestamped remediation ticket.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **primary_hash** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **index, bucket_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where uniques>1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **divergence_detected_at** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: bucket_id, divergence_detected_at, hashes, index, primary_hash, uniques.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heat-map of indexes × buckets over 30d (green/red); single value for '% of buckets verified'; time-chart of open divergences.
